reasoning_model_1: The audio content perfectly matches the script '你确定你真的没动过我的东西' with no errors. The fluency is excellent; the speaker uses a natural, conversational pace with appropriate breathing and prosody, indistinguishable from a human. Regarding the core paralinguistic feature 'Skepticism' (part of Cognitive State), the model performs exceptionally well. The speaker emphasizes key words like '确定' (sure) and '真的' (really) with a heavier, pressing tone (0.5s - 1.5s). The ending intonation maintains a high level of tension, effectively conveying deep suspicion and an interrogative stance. The emotion is vivid and spot-on.
reasoning_model_2: The audio content has a minor omission; the speaker says '你真没' instead of the requested '你真的没', omitting the particle '的'. The fluency is poor; the speech is stilted with a very unnatural, mechanical pause after '你确定' (0.8s), breaking the flow of the sentence. In terms of the core feature 'Skepticism', the performance is weak. The tone is flat and robotic, sounding more like a neutral TTS reading than a person expressing doubt. While there is a slight rising intonation at the end to indicate a question, the specific cognitive state of suspicion is not effectively conveyed due to the lack of emotional coloring and the mechanical delivery.
model_comparison: Model 1 is superior in all aspects. It accurately reproduces the text, maintains high naturalness, and vividly conveys the requested skeptical tone through appropriate stress and intonation. Model 2 fails to sound natural, has a minor content omission, and lacks the requested emotional expression, sounding robotic and neutral. Model 1 clearly wins.
